content.full_text:
November 13, 1990

Supervisor Maggie Erickson  
1740 Loma Drive  
Camarillo, CA 93010

Re: Your Request For Advice  
Our File No. A-90-648

Dear Supervisor Erickson:

This is in reply to your letter requesting advice concerning your obligation to disclose wedding gifts under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

What are the reporting requirements for wedding gifts?

CONCLUSION

You must report your community property interest in wedding gifts received by you and your spouse. The reporting threshold for gifts is $50. Therefore, you must report wedding gifts with a value of $100 or more.

ANALYSIS

An official must disclose on his or her Statement of Economic Interests, Form 721, any gift from a single source aggregating $50 or more. (Section 87207(a)(1).) However, wedding gifts are the property of both spouses unless they are peculiarly adaptable to the personal use of one spouse and unequivocally intended exclusively for the use of one spouse. (Regulation 18726.5, copy enclosed.) Therefore, because each spouse has a one-half interest in wedding gifts, only those gifts with a value of $100 or more are reportable. (In re Torres (1976) 2 FPPC Ops. 31; copy enclosed.)

1/ Government Code Section 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

If you have any questions or need further assistance, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[Signature]
by: Colleen McGee
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
